title: Why there will be military helicopters in Edinburgh on Tuesday

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Police and military will be seen at Murrayfield stadium on March 3 due to a training exercise (Image: Jane Barlow/PA) POLICE have warned the public not to be alarmed if they hear gunshots and see armed police or military helicopters in Edinburgh on Tuesday. Police Scotland will be working with the military at the capital’s Murrayfield stadium as part of a training exercise, named Exercise Kukri Dawn.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Led and managed by the RAF, the training began on February 23 and will last till March 13. But on March 3, armed policing will be involved in a firearms training scenario in Edinburgh. Police Scotland warned: “This means the public may see armed police officers and military personnel, as well as military helicopters, arriving at Murrayfield. READ MORE: BrewDog closes all bars for a day as sale announcement expected

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: “The public will also hear blank ammunition being used from around 1.30pm–2.30pm. “There will be no public access to Murrayfield during this time and nothing will be seen once those involved are placed inside the grounds.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Why locals may hear gunshots and see military helicopters in Edinburgh on Tuesday
